name: Real-Life Docking Panel v1
description: Sixteen CASF complexes curated for fast product-facing docking checks. The panel is grouped by common failure modes rather than by aggregate leaderboard value alone.

cases.title: Flat rigid aromatic fragment
cases.reason: Low-flexibility fragment-like ligand. Good for checking basic pose placement and aromatic packing.

cases.title: Small Zn-pocket fragment
cases.reason: Tiny ligand in a metal-containing site. Exposes overfitting to ligand size and brittle pocket centering.

cases.title: Compact bicyclic fragment
cases.reason: Rigid fragment with little torsional freedom. Useful for separating search failures from scoring failures.

cases.title: Charged fused-ring fragment
cases.reason: Small cationic fragment. Keeps the search space simple while still probing charge handling.

cases.title: Zn-aware medium ligand
cases.reason: Coordination-sensitive site where generic hydrophobic scoring often looks better than it should.

cases.title: Mg-bound aromatic binder
cases.reason: Mixed aromatic and metal-mediated recognition. Good stress case for explicit interaction terms.

cases.title: Mg-assisted larger pocket
cases.reason: Bigger ligand in a metal-containing site. Helps catch missed coordination in otherwise plausible poses.

cases.title: Ca-containing aromatic binder
cases.reason: Tests whether non-Zn metals are treated consistently during search and ranking.

cases.title: Peptidic charged ligand
cases.reason: High torsional freedom plus formal charge. A good quick readout for protonation and conformational search.

cases.title: Long dicationic binder
cases.reason: Flexible, highly charged ligand that punishes weak electrostatics and weak exploration.

cases.title: Even longer dicationic analogue
cases.reason: Near-neighbor to 1h22 with more flexibility. Good for stability and ranking consistency checks.

cases.title: Charged aromatic medium ligand
cases.reason: Balanced charge plus aromaticity. Useful when rigid fragments pass but realistic ligands still drift.

cases.title: Bulky aromatic high-affinity ligand
cases.reason: Large, lipophilic ligand with many torsions. A practical stress test for exploration versus collapse.

cases.title: Very large aromatic scaffold
cases.reason: High-MW, ring-rich ligand. Good for runtime, clustering, and large-ligand ranking behavior.

cases.title: Bulky heteroatom-rich ligand
cases.reason: Large ligand with more polar functionality than the purely lipophilic cases.

cases.title: Large charged aromatic binder
cases.reason: Large aromatic ligand with formal charge. Useful for checking whether heavy hydrophobics swamp electrostatics.
